justification: The paper presents a clear residual-based validation workflow that convincingly confirms continuity on the dataset, but the central claim of ‘compressible Euler’ dynamics rests mainly on moderate correlations with a pressure-gradient proxy and lacks coefficient estimation, alternative EOS tests, or predictive validation. Major methodological gaps—unspecified data provenance and scales, reliance on first-order temporal differencing with Δt effectively assumed, testing a nonconservative momentum form, limited and simplified viscous modeling, and missing spectral/dealiasing details—undermine the strength of the momentum-law identification. The mathematical audit finds only minor issues (a 1283 vs 128^3 typo; otherwise consistent), but the numerical audit could not reproduce results and many robustness checks are absent, limiting evidence quality. Overall, the work is promising as a diagnostic framework under sparse time sampling but requires substantial additions and clarifications to support its strongest conclusions and to establish novelty over existing PDE discovery/validation methods.
